key_insight: Compass improves prostate cancer detection by analyzing micro-ultrasound as a multi-view video stream rather than isolated frames, capturing spatial and rotational context.
clinical_relevance: The approach could help complement expert micro-ultrasound reading by better estimating cancer risk during prostate biopsy workflows.
method: Models continuous rotational sweep videos and biopsy-time micro-ultrasound frames together
Uses a transformer conditioned on probe rotational angle to aggregate evidence across the study
Outputs both frame-level and study-level risk scores
evidence_signal: The model was trained and tested on a multi-center clinical trial dataset and compared against published AI baselines and expert risk scores.
watch_item: Performance may depend on consistent video acquisition, probe angle recording, and access to multi-center data similar to the training setting.
paper_title: Compass: Prostate Cancer Detection Needs Multi-View Context
